prompt:
Design a Workforce Compliance & Inspection Operations Platform to manage employer job postings, field inspections, result publication, objections, and governed communications in a fully offline environment. The backend uses Laravel to expose resource-scoped APIs for Authentication, Employer, Job, Inspection, Result Publication, Objection/Ticketing, Messaging/Reminders, Content Publishing, and Approval Workflow Engine, with MySQL persisting all records and audit trails. Roles include System Administrator, Compliance Reviewer, Employer Manager, Inspector, and General User (e.g., applicant/participant). Employer APIs support create/update of company qualification profiles and document attachments, plus admin review decisions; employers remain “pending” until approved, with decisions required within 3 business days and mandatory reason codes for rejection. Job APIs support create/edit and online/offline controls with validation rules: salaryMin/salaryMax in USD integers, salaryMax must be ≥ salaryMin, educationLevel enum, workLocation as structured US address fields, and category/tag constraints; duplicate detection blocks postings with the same employerId + normalized title + ZIP code within 30 days, and rate limiting caps publication to 20 new jobs per employer per 24 hours. Result Publication APIs support phased release (draft, internal, public) with configurable field masking (e.g., lastName masked to first initial, phone masked to last 4) and versioned public snapshots; Objection APIs allow submissions within 7 calendar days of public release, accept supplemental files (PDF/JPG/PNG, max 10 MB each, max 5 files), route through ticket states (intake, review, adjudication), and sync adjudication conclusions into the final published result version. Messaging/Reminders APIs manage subscription preferences and generate in-app messages for events (check-in reminders 24 hours and 2 hours prior, change/cancellation notices, refund/adjustment outcomes where applicable), with per-user read status and retention of 365 days.

Data models in MySQL must include immutable audit tables for workflow actions and result/objection decisions, storing actorId, role, timestamp, priorValueHash/newValueHash, and human-readable reason; primary entities include Users (unique username, password hash, disabled flag), Sessions (deviceId, lastSeenAt), Employers, EmployerQualifications, Jobs, Inspections, OfflineSyncBatches, ResultVersions, Objections, Tickets, Messages, ContentItems, and WorkflowDefinitions/WorkflowInstances. The Approval Workflow Engine APIs support workflow definition versioning, nodes with conditional branches, parallel approvals (all-approve or any-approve), reassignment, and timeouts; default timeout is 48 hours with one auto-escalation to a configured supervisor role and a required escalation note, while all workflow instance logs are append-only. Inspection offline mode requires local caching of assigned records, queued uploads with resumable transfers (chunk size 2–5 MB), idempotency keys for every upload, conflict detection using record version numbers plus updatedAt, server-side merge rules favoring the latest confirmed adjudication fields, and sync logs with automatic retry (exponential backoff, max 8 attempts) and quarantine after repeated failure. Security requires username/password only (min 12 chars, complexity rules, lockout after 10 failed attempts for 15 minutes), encryption at rest for sensitive columns (PII fields) and masking in responses based on role and family/member visibility scopes. Non-functional requirements target a single-node Docker deployment, 99th percentile API latency under 300 ms for typical queries at 50 concurrent users, structured log aggregation and trace correlation IDs stored locally, health and capacity checks (disk <15% free triggers alert), and configuration/gray-release toggles implemented as local DB-backed flags without any external services.
frontend_language: None (API-only, no frontend in this repo)
frontend_framework: None (external clients will consume the API)
database: MySQL (single-node, full audit trails and offline-sync support)
